title: Study Flow - Get It Working
description: Fix the critical path so Trey can actually study. Entry card -> Priming methods -> Tutor chat -> Notes.

stories.title: Priming method cards use colorful box UI with name and description
stories.description: The Priming panel method selector currently shows plain buttons. Replace with colorful card-style boxes like the Methods page (/methods or the method library UI). Each method card should show: (1) Colored background or left-accent bar (each method gets a distinct color from the theme palette). (2) Method name in bold. (3) One-line description/summary of what the method does. (4) Checkbox or selected state indicator so user can pick one or more methods. Look at the Methods page components for reference -- there may be existing card components in the codebase that render methods with colors. Search for MethodCard, MethodTile, or similar in the components directory. The Priming panel method list is rendered in the studio priming content -- trace from TutorShell primingStudioContent through to wherever methods are listed.
acceptanceCriteria: Each method shows as a colored card with name and description
Methods have distinct colors (not all the same)
User can select one or more methods
Selected methods have a visual selected state (border highlight or check)
dev-browser: verify method cards have background colors and description text

stories.title: Priming method run returns actual results instead of empty artifacts
stories.description: When user selects a method (e.g. Learning Objectives Primer) and clicks Run, the method executes but the Output Area shows 'This run completed, but no study artifacts were returned for the selected output format.' This means the backend returned a response but the frontend is not parsing it correctly, OR the backend method runner is not producing output in the expected format. Debug this by: (1) Check the network request when Run is clicked -- what does the API return? (2) Check what format the frontend expects for method results. (3) Fix the mismatch so method results actually display. The backend API is at 127.0.0.1:5000. The method runner endpoint is likely POST /api/tutor/run or similar. Check brain/dashboard/api_tutor.py for the endpoint.
acceptanceCriteria: Running a method produces visible output in the Output Area
Method Result section shows actual content (not 'no study artifacts' message)
Output is formatted and readable (markdown or structured text)
dev-browser: click Run on a method, wait for response, verify output area has content

stories.title: Priming Chat is functional instead of coming soon placeholder
stories.description: The Priming Chat section shows 'Chat with priming results coming soon' -- this is a placeholder. Replace with a real chat input that lets the user refine priming results by chatting. The chat should: (1) Have a text input and send button. (2) Send messages to the backend priming chat endpoint. (3) Show the conversation history (user messages + assistant responses). (4) Use the current priming context (method results, selected materials) as context for the chat. If the backend endpoint does not exist yet, create a simple one that takes the priming context + user message and returns an LLM response. Check if there is already a priming chat endpoint in api_tutor.py or a separate priming API file.
acceptanceCriteria: Priming Chat section has a text input and send button
User can type a message and send it
Sent message appears in chat history
Response from backend appears in chat history
Chat uses priming context (method results) for responses
dev-browser: type in priming chat, send, verify response appears

stories.title: Tutor chat panel sends messages and receives LLM responses
stories.description: The Tutor panel must be able to send a message and receive a response from the LLM backend. Open the Tutor panel. Type a message in the chat input. Click send or press Enter. Verify the message appears in the chat history and a response comes back. If the chat does not work, trace the API call and fix it. The backend tutor chat endpoint is in brain/dashboard/api_tutor.py. Make sure the tutor has access to the primed context (materials, method results) when generating responses.
acceptanceCriteria: Tutor panel has a chat input and send button
User can type a message and send it
Sent message appears in chat history
Response from LLM appears in chat history
dev-browser: open Tutor panel, type message, send, verify response
